description: Comprehensive integration test suite for the Mento Protocol v2 → v3 upgrade. Validates on-chain state after all 7 upgrade scripts and tests critical user flows (swaps, rebalancing, CDP, minting/burning, oracles).

userStories.title: Create base test harness with address resolution
userStories.description: As a developer, I want a base abstract test contract that forks a chain and resolves all key V3 contract addresses from the Treb registry so that all other test files can inherit from it.
acceptanceCriteria: Create test/integration/V3IntegrationBase.t.sol abstract contract inheriting from forge-std/Test.sol and ProxyHelper
setUp() forks a chain via vm.createFork using FORK_URL env var

setUp() resolves key addresses from the Treb registry via lookupOrFail/lookupProxyOrFail: FPMMFactory, OracleAdapter, FactoryRegistry, VirtualPoolFactory, Router, ReserveV2, ReserveLiquidityStrategy, CDPLiquidityStrategy, BreakerBox, SortedOracles, ProxyAdmin, MarketHoursBreaker, Broker, ReserveSafe
setUp() loads config via Config.get()
Expose helper methods: _mintToken(address token, address to, uint256 amount) using deal(), _getOwner() returning the migration owner address
All addresses stored as internal state variables accessible to child contracts
forge build succeeds with no errors
userStories.notes: This is the foundation — every other test file inherits from this. Use the same lookup pattern as the deployment scripts (lookupOrFail, lookupProxyOrFail). The tests should work against any network the fork points to (Celo mainnet, Celo Sepolia, local Anvil with upgrade applied).

userStories.title: Test proxy implementations and init protection
userStories.description: As a developer, I want to verify all V3 proxies point to correct implementations and that implementation contracts cannot be re-initialized.
acceptanceCriteria: Create test/integration/StateVerification.t.sol inheriting V3IntegrationBase
Test OracleAdapter, FPMMFactory, FactoryRegistry, ReserveV2, ReserveLiquidityStrategy, CDPLiquidityStrategy proxy → implementation mappings using verifyProxyImpl or getProxyImplementation
Test that calling initialize() on each implementation contract reverts (init disabled)
Test ProxyAdmin is set correctly on FPMM pool proxies (iterate pools from FPMMFactory)
forge build succeeds
userStories.notes: Use lookupOrFail to get implementation addresses with the v3.0.0 label. Use ProxyHelper.getProxyImplementation() for proxy checks.

userStories.title: Test ownership and access control
userStories.description: As a developer, I want to verify all V3 contracts are owned by the correct multisig and that non-owners are rejected.
acceptanceCriteria: Add tests to StateVerification.t.sol for ownership of: OracleAdapter, FPMMFactory, FactoryRegistry, VirtualPoolFactory, ReserveV2, ReserveLiquidityStrategy, CDPLiquidityStrategy using IOwnable(addr).owner()
Test that a random non-owner address calling transferOwnership on each contract reverts
Test that a random address calling FPMMFactory.deployFPMM() reverts (only owner)
forge build succeeds
userStories.notes: The owner should be the migrationOwner address. Use vm.prank(randomAddress) to test access denial.

userStories.title: Test OracleAdapter and BreakerBox configuration
userStories.description: As a developer, I want to verify oracle infrastructure wiring: OracleAdapter → SortedOracles, BreakerBox has MarketHoursBreaker on FX feeds.
acceptanceCriteria: Add tests to StateVerification.t.sol verifying OracleAdapter.sortedOracles() == SortedOracles proxy
Test OracleAdapter.breakerBox() == BreakerBox address
Test OracleAdapter.marketHoursBreaker() == deployed MarketHoursBreaker
Test BreakerBox.isBreaker(marketHoursBreaker) == true and breakerTradingMode == 3
Test MarketHoursBreaker is enabled on every FX rate feed ID from config.getFxRateFeedIds()
Test OracleAdapter.getFXRateIfValid returns non-zero values for each configured feed
forge build succeeds
userStories.notes: Use config.getFxRateFeedIds() to iterate all FX feeds dynamically.

userStories.title: Test FPMMFactory, FactoryRegistry, and Router state
userStories.description: As a developer, I want to verify the factory and router infrastructure is correctly initialized.
acceptanceCriteria: Add tests to StateVerification.t.sol verifying FPMMFactory.oracleAdapter() == OracleAdapter proxy
Test FPMMFactory.proxyAdmin() == ProxyAdmin
Test FPMMFactory default params match config: lpFee, protocolFee, protocolFeeRecipient, feeSetter, rebalanceIncentive, rebalanceThresholdAbove, rebalanceThresholdBelow
Test FPMMFactory.isRegisteredImplementation(fpmmImpl) == true
Test FactoryRegistry.fallbackPoolFactory() == FPMMFactory proxy
Test FactoryRegistry.isPoolFactoryApproved(fpmmFactory) == true
Test Router.factoryRegistry() == FactoryRegistry proxy and Router.defaultFactory() == FPMMFactory proxy
forge build succeeds
userStories.notes: Get expected default params from config.getDefaultFPMMParams(). Get fpmmImpl via lookupOrFail('FPMM:v3.0.0').

userStories.title: Test ReserveV2 configuration
userStories.description: As a developer, I want to verify ReserveV2 has correct asset registrations and spender permissions.
acceptanceCriteria: Add tests to StateVerification.t.sol verifying ReserveSafe is registered as other reserve address and reserve manager spender on ReserveV2
Test ReserveLiquidityStrategy is registered as liquidity strategy spender
Test that a random address calling registerStableAsset() reverts (owner-only)
forge build succeeds
userStories.notes: Use IReserveV2 interface for isOtherReserveAddress, isReserveManagerSpender, isLiquidityStrategySpender checks.

userStories.title: Test FPMM pool state for all deployed pools
userStories.description: As a developer, I want to verify each FPMM pool from config has correct tokens, oracle config, params, and liquidity.
acceptanceCriteria: Create test/integration/FPMMPoolState.t.sol inheriting V3IntegrationBase
Iterate FPMM pools from config.getFPMMConfigs() and for each: verify token0/token1 match sorted pair, referenceRateFeedID and invertRateFeed match config, oracleAdapter == OracleAdapter proxy
Verify FPMM params (lpFee, protocolFee, protocolFeeRecipient, feeSetter, rebalanceIncentive, thresholds) match config
Verify pool has non-zero reserves (r0 > 0, r1 > 0) and non-zero LP totalSupply
Verify FPMMFactory.isPool(fpmmProxy) == true for each pool
forge build succeeds
userStories.notes: Use FPMMFactory.getPool(token0, token1) to look up each pool address. Use IFPMM interface for state queries.

userStories.title: Test VirtualPool deployment
userStories.description: As a developer, I want to verify virtual pools exist for all configured exchange pairs with correct tokens.
acceptanceCriteria: Add tests (either in FPMMPoolState.t.sol or StateVerification.t.sol) that iterate BiPoolManager exchanges marked createVirtual in config
Verify VirtualPoolFactory.getPool(token0, token1) returns a non-zero address for each
Verify virtual pool token0/token1 match the underlying exchange pair (sorted)
forge build succeeds
userStories.notes: Use config.getExchangeConfig() and BiPoolManager.getExchangeIds()/getPoolExchange() to enumerate exchanges, similar to DeployVirtualPools.s.sol.

userStories.title: Test CDP migration state: roles and strategy config
userStories.description: As a developer, I want to verify CDP migration is complete — V2 exchange destroyed, Liquity roles set, CDP strategy configured.
acceptanceCriteria: Create test/integration/CDPMigrationVerification.t.sol inheriting V3IntegrationBase
For each CDP-migrated token: test the V2 BiPoolManager exchange no longer exists for that pair
Test Broker is NOT minter/burner on CDP-migrated debt token
Test Liquity contracts have correct roles: BorrowerOperations+ActivePool are minters; CollateralRegistry+BorrowerOperations+TroveManager+StabilityPool are burners; StabilityPool is operator
Test CDPLiquidityStrategy is enabled on the FPMM and FPMM is registered on CDPLiquidityStrategy
Test CDPConfig values (stabilityPool, collateralRegistry, stabilityPoolPercentage, maxIterations) match config
Test pool config (cooldown, incentives, protocolFeeRecipient) matches config
forge build succeeds
userStories.notes: Use CDPMigrationConfigLib.get(tokenSymbol) to load per-token CDP config. Use IAddressesRegistry for Liquity contract addresses.

userStories.title: Test CDP migration state: reserve trove and cleanup
userStories.description: As a developer, I want to verify the reserve trove is active with correct parameters and that factory permissions are cleaned up.
acceptanceCriteria: Add to CDPMigrationVerification.t.sol: test FXPriceFeed.rateFeedID matches config for each CDP-migrated token
Test reserve trove is active (status == active) with correct interest rate matching config
Test reserve trove NFT is owned by reserveTroveManager (ReserveSafe)
Test reserve trove debt >= debt token total supply
Test reserve trove has non-zero collateral
Test ReserveTroveFactory does NOT have minter/burner roles (cleanup verified)
forge build succeeds
userStories.notes: Use ITroveManager from bold/contracts for trove queries. Use registry.troveManager() to get the TroveManager address.

userStories.title: Test FPMM direct swap flow
userStories.description: As a user, I want to swap tokens directly through an FPMM pool in both directions and verify balances update correctly.
acceptanceCriteria: Create test/integration/FPMMSwap.t.sol inheriting V3IntegrationBase
For at least one FPMM pool: test swapping token0 → token1 — deal tokens to test user, transfer to pool, call swap(), verify user receives amountOut and balances change
Test swapping token1 → token0 on the same pool
Test getAmountOut preview matches actual swap output
Test swap updates pool reserves correctly (getReserves before/after)
forge build succeeds
userStories.notes: Use deal() to mint tokens to a test address. Pattern: deal tokens → transfer to FPMM → fpmm.swap(). Use vm.prank for the test user.

userStories.title: Test Router swap flow
userStories.description: As a user, I want to swap tokens through the Router and verify it routes through the correct pool.
acceptanceCriteria: Create test/integration/RouterSwap.t.sol inheriting V3IntegrationBase
Test Router.swap() successfully routes a token0 → token1 swap through the correct FPMM pool
Verify the user receives tokens and balances update correctly
Test Router correctly resolves the pool via FactoryRegistry
forge build succeeds
userStories.notes: Check the IRouter interface for the exact swap function signature. The Router looks up pools via FactoryRegistry → FPMMFactory.

userStories.title: Test ReserveLiquidityStrategy rebalancing
userStories.description: As a keeper, I want to trigger rebalancing on FPMM pools via ReserveLiquidityStrategy and verify price difference reduces.
acceptanceCriteria: Create test/integration/RebalanceReserve.t.sol inheriting V3IntegrationBase
For an FPMM pool with ReserveLiquidityStrategy: do a large one-sided swap to push pool out of balance
Call rebalance() and verify price difference (from getRebalancingState) decreases
Test rebalance respects cooldown: calling again immediately reverts or is no-op
Test rebalance when pool is already within threshold is no-op or reverts
forge build succeeds
userStories.notes: Pattern from CreateFPMM._verifyRebalance: large swap → log rebalancing state → rebalance() → verify newPriceDifference < oldPriceDifference. Use vm.warp to test cooldown.

userStories.title: Test CDPLiquidityStrategy rebalancing
userStories.description: As a keeper, I want to trigger rebalancing on CDP-backed FPMM pools via CDPLiquidityStrategy.
acceptanceCriteria: Create test/integration/RebalanceCDP.t.sol inheriting V3IntegrationBase
For a CDP-backed FPMM pool: do a large one-sided swap to imbalance the pool
Call CDPLiquidityStrategy.rebalance(fpmm) and verify price difference decreases
Test cooldown is respected: calling rebalance again too soon reverts
forge build succeeds
userStories.notes: Similar pattern to US-013 but using CDPLiquidityStrategy. The CDP strategy interacts with StabilityPool and CollateralRegistry.

userStories.title: Test stable token minting and burning roles
userStories.description: As a protocol, I want to verify correct contracts can mint/burn stable tokens and unauthorized callers are rejected.
acceptanceCriteria: Create test/integration/StableTokenRoles.t.sol inheriting V3IntegrationBase
For a CDP-migrated token: test that BorrowerOperations (via vm.prank) can call mint, and a random address cannot
Test that authorized burners (CollateralRegistry, BorrowerOperations, TroveManager, StabilityPool) can burn
Test that Broker is NOT able to mint/burn on CDP-migrated tokens
Test that StabilityPool as operator can transferFrom without approval on CDP-migrated tokens
forge build succeeds
userStories.notes: Use IStableTokenV3 interface. Use vm.prank to impersonate each authorized contract. deal() tokens for burn tests.

userStories.title: Test oracle and MarketHoursBreaker behavior
userStories.description: As a developer, I want to verify MarketHoursBreaker halts trading outside FX hours and OracleAdapter returns valid rates during market hours.
acceptanceCriteria: Create test/integration/OracleBreaker.t.sol inheriting V3IntegrationBase
Test that during FX market hours OracleAdapter.getFXRateIfValid returns non-zero values for all configured feeds
Test BreakerBox correctly reports trading mode for MarketHoursBreaker
If toggleable breaker: use vm.warp to simulate outside market hours and verify breaker triggers
forge build succeeds
userStories.notes: FX market hours are Mon-Fri. Use vm.warp to move to a weekend timestamp to test breaker triggering. Check MARKET_HOURS_BREAKER_TOGGLEABLE env var.

userStories.title: Test FPMM liquidity provision and removal
userStories.description: As an LP, I want to add and remove liquidity from FPMM pools and verify LP tokens and reserves update correctly.
acceptanceCriteria: Create test/integration/LiquidityProvision.t.sol inheriting V3IntegrationBase
Test providing liquidity: deal both tokens to user, transfer to FPMM, call mint(), verify LP tokens received and reserves increased
Test removing liquidity: transfer LP tokens back to FPMM, call burn(), verify tokens returned and reserves decreased proportionally
Test LP token balance reflects proportional share of pool
forge build succeeds
userStories.notes: Pattern from CreateFPMM._mintInitialLiquidity: transfer token0 + token1 to FPMM → fpmm.mint(recipient). For burn: transfer LP to FPMM → fpmm.burn(recipient).

userStories.title: Test reserve trove operations and interest accrual
userStories.description: As a developer, I want to verify the reserve trove is healthy and accrues interest over time.
acceptanceCriteria: Create test/integration/ReserveTrove.t.sol inheriting V3IntegrationBase
Test reserve trove is active with correct collateral and debt values (non-zero)
Test trove collateralization ratio meets the configured minimum
Test interest accrual: record debt, vm.warp forward 30 days, call getLatestTroveData, verify debt increased
forge build succeeds
userStories.notes: Use ITroveManager.getLatestTroveData(troveId) for trove state. The trove ID can be found via the ReserveTroveFactory or by iterating troveManager.

userStories.title: Test CDP operations: opening troves and StabilityPool deposits
userStories.description: As a user, I want to open a new trove and deposit into the StabilityPool on a CDP-migrated token.
acceptanceCriteria: Create test/integration/CDPOperations.t.sol inheriting V3IntegrationBase
Test opening a new trove: deal collateral to user, approve BorrowerOperations, call openTrove with valid CR and interest rate, verify trove is active and user received debt tokens
Test depositing debt tokens into StabilityPool: approve and call provideToSP(), verify deposit registered
Test withdrawing from StabilityPool: call withdrawFromSP(), verify tokens returned
forge build succeeds
userStories.notes: Use IBorrowerOperations for openTrove. Use IStabilityPool for provideToSP/withdrawFromSP. Deal collateral tokens (from registry.collToken()) to the test user. Ensure the trove meets minimum collateralization requirements.

userStories.title: Test CDP operations: liquidations and interest collection
userStories.description: As a developer, I want to verify trove liquidation mechanics and interest collection work on CDP-migrated tokens.
acceptanceCriteria: Add to CDPOperations.t.sol: test liquidating an undercollateralized trove — open trove, manipulate oracle price to make it undercollateralized, call liquidate, verify trove status changes
Test interest collection: open trove, warp time forward, verify accrued interest can be collected by the system
Test that StabilityPool depositors receive collateral gains after a liquidation
forge build succeeds
userStories.notes: For liquidation tests: open a trove at minimum CR, then use a mock aggregator or vm.store to lower the collateral price below liquidation threshold, then call troveManager.liquidate(). Check IStabilityPool for collateral gain claims.
